verses.text: The word comes a second time, and Jonah goes to Nineveh, 'an exceeding great city.' A renewed call. God graciously commissions His servant again after his failure.
The word comes a second time, and Jonah goes to Nineveh, 'an exceeding great city.' A renewed call. God graciously commissions His servant again after his failure.
The word comes a second time, and Jonah goes to Nineveh, 'an exceeding great city.' A renewed call. God graciously commissions His servant again after his failure.
He cries, 'Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown,' and the people believe God, fasting in sackcloth. Swift repentance. A pagan city humbles itself at one warning.
He cries, 'Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown,' and the people believe God, fasting in sackcloth. Swift repentance. A pagan city humbles itself at one warning.
The king rises from his throne, dons sackcloth, and decrees all turn from violence; 'who can tell if God will turn?' Humble hope. Even kings bow, casting themselves on possible mercy.

The king rises from his throne, dons sackcloth, and decrees all turn from violence; 'who can tell if God will turn?' Humble hope. Even kings bow, casting themselves on possible mercy.
The king rises from his throne, dons sackcloth, and decrees all turn from violence; 'who can tell if God will turn?' Humble hope. Even kings bow, casting themselves on possible mercy.
God sees their works, that they turned, and 'repented of the evil... and he did it not.' Mercy triumphs. God spares the truly penitent, however great their sin.
